DSBUFFERDESC

Describes the characteristics of a new DirectSoundBuffer object. Used by the IDirectSound8::CreateSoundBuffer method.

typedef struct { 
    DWORD dwSize; 
    DWORD dwFlags; 
    DWORD dwBufferBytes; 
    LPWAVEFORMATEX lpwfxFormat; 
    LPCDSMIXBINS lpMixBins;
    DWORD dwInputMixBin;
} DSBUFFERDESC, *LPDSBUFFERDESC; 
 
typedef const DSBUFFERDESC *LPCDSBUFFERDESC;

Members

dwSize
Size of the structure, in bytes.
dwFlags
Flags specifying the capabilities to include when creating a new DirectSoundBuffer object. The following values are defined.
Value Description
DSBCAPS_CTRL3D The buffer will be a 3D HRTF buffer. Can only be set for buffers with a mono format.
DSBCAPS_CTRLPOSITIONNOTIFY The buffer must have position notification capability.
DSBCAPS_FXIN The buffer is used as the destination of a post-effects submix operation. When specifying this flag, the lpwfxFormat parameter must be null.
DSBCAPS_FXIN2 The buffer is used as the destination of a post-effects submix operation. Buffers of this type cannot be passed to SetOutputBuffer. Instead, the application is expected to call Play (PlayEx) and Stop (StopEx) on the destination voice. When specifying this flag, the lpwfxFormat parameter must be null.
DSBCAPS_LOCDEFER The buffer will not be assigned a hardware voice until it is played. If this flag is not set, the hardware voice is permanently allocated to this buffer or stream.
DSBCAPS_MIXIN The buffer must be capable of receiving data from other buffers. The value of lpwfxFormat should be set to NULL. For buffers created with DSBCAPS_MIXIN, SetFrequency, Lock, and SetBufferData will fail. MIXIN buffers only receive data from other DirectSound buffers, streams, or effects chains.
DSBCAPS_MUTE3DATMAXDISTANCE The buffer is muted and no rolloff calculations are performed if the distance from the listener meets or exceeds the buffer's MaxDistance value.

The following flags are ignored by DirectSound on Xbox. They are included solely to allow easier porting of existing code:

DSBCAPS_CTRLFREQUENCY
DSBCAPS_CTRLVOLUME

dwBufferBytes
Size of the new buffer, in bytes. If this value is 0, DirectSound will not allocate any memory for this DirectSound Buffer. In this case, the application should use IDirectSoundBuffer8::SetBufferData to tell DirectSound where the sound buffer data, allocated by the application, resides.

This value must be 0 when creating a buffer with the DSBCAPS_MIXIN or DSBCAPS_FXIN flag. For other buffers, the minimum and maximum sizes allowed are specified by DSBSIZE_MIN and DSBSIZE_MAX, defined in Dsound.h.

lpwfxFormat
Address of a WAVEFORMATEX structure specifying the waveform format for the buffer.
lpMixBins
Address of a DSMIXBINS structure that contains the output mixbin identifiers and volume pairs. Can be set to NULL, which specifies that the default mixbin volume pairs are used. (See Mixbin Volume Pair Combinations for more information about the default mixbin volume pairs.)
dwInputMixBin
Value that contains the identifier for the input mixbin for FXIN buffers only, zero otherwise.

Remarks

Because DSBCAPS_MIXIN, DSBCAPS_FXIN, and DSBCAPS_FXIN2 buffers are used to receive audio data instead of generating it, the following settings are required:

  • The dwBufferBytes member must be set to 0.
  • The dwFlags member cannot include the DSBCAPS_LOCDEFER or DSBCAPS_CTRLPOSITIONNOTIFY flags. DSBCAPS_FXIN2 buffers are an exception to this and can include the DSBCAPS_LOCDEFER flag.
  • The lpwfxFormat member must be set to NULL.

Buffers created with either the DSBCAPS_MIXIN or DSBCAPS_FXIN flags automatically start playing when the first source voice starts and stop when the last source voice stops.

Buffers created with the DSBCAPS_FXIN2 flag cannot be passed to the SetOutputBuffer function. Instead, the application must explicitly call Play (PlayEx) and Stop (StopEx) on the destination voice for buffers of this type. Calls to SetPitch will have no effect on the destination voice. Doppler shifting of a 3D FXIN2 buffer will also have no effect.

Requirements

  Header: Declared in Dsound.h.

See Also

IDirectSound8::CreateSoundBuffer, DirectSound Buffers
